summary: This cycle says the major labs are shipping agent infrastructure faster than base models: Anthropic launched self-improving 'dreaming' agents with MCP tunnels and self-hosted sandboxes, Mistral shipped remote coding agents and a 128B workhorse model, Google I/O unveiled Gemini 4.0 and agentic Android, and Alibaba turned 4 billion products into a conversational agentic shopping experience. The deeper pattern is that agentic AI is becoming async, on-device, and enterprise-governed all at once.

highlights.title: yetone/native-feel-skill
highlights.summary: An Agent Skill for designing cross-platform desktop apps that feel native — distilled from Raycast's 2.0 deep-dive and reverse engineering of Raycast Beta.app. Eight architectural tenets, four-layer architecture, WebKit/WebView2 survival guide, 75-item ship audit.
highlights.why: Agent UX is moving beyond chat boxes — interface design is becoming part of the research frontier.

highlights.title: HermannBjorgvin/Clawdmeter
highlights.summary: ESP32 desk dashboard that shows Claude Code usage
highlights.why: This is playful but telling: once people start building physical dashboards for an agent, it usually means the tool has become part of their everyday workflow rather than a one-off demo.

highlights.title: Claude Managed Agents gets 'Dreaming' self-improvement, MCP tunnels, and self-hosted sandboxes
highlights.summary: Anthropic shipped two major privacy/security features for Claude Managed Agents: MCP tunnels and self-hosted sandboxes. Earlier in May, 'Dreaming' was introduced — enabling agents to self-improve over time. Harvey reported a 6× jump in task completion.
highlights.why: This is the clearest signal yet that agent infrastructure is outpacing model releases: self-improvement, secure tunnels, and private sandboxes are the new battleground.

highlights.title: Google I/O 2026: Gemini 4.0, agentic coding tools, and Gemini Spark AI agent
highlights.summary: Google I/O 2026 kicks off with expected announcements including Gemini 4.0, agentic coding tools, the Gemini Spark AI agent, Android XR glasses, and deep Gemini Intelligence integration into Android.
highlights.why: When a major platform bets its annual developer conference on agents, it signals that agentic interfaces are becoming a first-class platform primitive, not just a feature.

highlights.title: Claude for Small Business
highlights.why: This is one of the clearest signs that agentic workflows are being packaged for normal businesses, not just developers — connectors and ready-to-run automations are becoming the product.

highlights.title: Show HN: Needle: We Distilled Gemini Tool Calling into a 26M Model
highlights.summary: Hey HN, Henry here from Cactus. We open-sourced Needle, a 26M parameter function-calling (tool use) model. It runs at 6000 tok/s prefill and 1200 tok/s decode on consumer devices. We were always frustrated by the little effort made towards building agentic models that run on budget phones, so we conducted investigations that led to an observation: agentic experiences are built upon tool calling, and massive models are overkill for it. Tool calling is fundamentally retrieval-and-assembly (match query to tool name, extract argument values, emit JSON), not reasoning. Cross-attention is the right primitive for this, and FFN parameters are wasted at this scale. Simple Attention Networks: the entire model is just attention and gating, no MLPs anywhere. Needle is an experimental run for single-shot function calling for consumer devices (phones, watches, glasses...). Training: - Pretrained on 200B tokens across 16 TPU v6e (27 hours) - Post-trained on 2B tokens of synthesized function-calling data (45 minutes) - Dataset synthesized via Gemini with 15 tool categories (timers, messaging, navigation, smart home, etc.) You can test it right now and finetune on your Mac/PC: https://github.com/cactus-compute/needle The full writeup on the architecture is here: https://github.com/cactus-compute/needle/blob/main/docs/simp... We found that the "no FFN" finding generalizes beyond function calling to any task where the model has access to external structured knowledge (RAG, tool use, retrieval-augmented generation). The model doesn't need to memorize facts in FFN weights if the facts are provided in the input. Experimental results to published. While it beats FunctionGemma-270M, Qwen-0.6B, Granite-350M, LFM2.5-350M on single-shot function calling, those models have more scope/capacity and excel in conversational settings. We encourage you to test on your own tools via the playground and finetune accordingly. This is part of our broader work on Cactus ( https://github.com/cactus-compute/cactus ), an inference engine built from scratch for mobile, wearables and custom hardware. We wrote about Cactus here previously: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=44524544 Everything is MIT licensed. Weights: https://huggingface.co/Cactus-Compute/needle GitHub: https://github.com/cactus-compute/needle
highlights.why: A 26M-parameter function-calling model is a real compression milestone: useful tool use is getting small enough to run on tiny devices, not just cloud GPUs.

highlights.title: Mistral Medium 3.5 and remote coding agents in Vibe
highlights.summary: Mistral launched Medium 3.5 (128B dense, 256k context, 77.6% SWE-Bench Verified) alongside remote coding agents in Vibe — async cloud-based sessions — and a new Work mode in Le Chat for complex agentic tasks.
highlights.why: Remote/async coding agents are a meaningful shift: agents are moving from synchronous chat sessions to long-running background workers that can operate across hours.

highlights.title: Alibaba integrates Qwen AI with Taobao for agentic shopping across 4+ billion products
highlights.summary: Alibaba integrated Qwen AI with Taobao and Tmall, enabling end-to-end agentic shopping — handling search, logistics, customer service, and after-sales via conversational AI rather than keyword search.
highlights.why: This is the most concrete 'agent replaces app' example at scale: 4 billion products accessible conversationally, with real transactions and fulfillment behind it.

items.title: Claude Managed Agents gets 'Dreaming', MCP tunnels, and self-hosted sandboxes
items.summary: MCP tunnels and self-hosted sandboxes for privacy; 'Dreaming' enables self-improving agents with 6× task completion gains.
items.why: This is the clearest signal yet that agent infrastructure is outpacing model releases.

items.title: Google I/O 2026: Gemini 4.0, agentic coding, Spark AI agent
items.summary: Expected: Gemini 4.0, agentic coding tools, Gemini Spark AI agent, Android XR glasses, and Gemini Intelligence for Android.
items.why: When a major platform bets its annual developer conference on agents, it signals that agentic interfaces are becoming a first-class platform primitive.

items.title: Claude for Small Business
items.why: This is one of the clearest signs that agentic workflows are being packaged for normal businesses, not just developers — connectors and ready-to-run automations are becoming the product.

items.title: Mistral Medium 3.5 and remote coding agents in Vibe
items.summary: 128B dense model with 256k context and 77.6% SWE-Bench Verified; async remote coding agents and Work mode in Le Chat.
items.why: Remote/async coding agents are a meaningful shift toward long-running background workers.

items.title: Alibaba Qwen integrated with Taobao for agentic shopping
items.summary: End-to-end agentic shopping across 4+ billion products via conversational AI.
items.why: The most concrete 'agent replaces app' example at scale, with real transactions behind it.

items.title: Codex is now in the ChatGPT mobile app
items.why: The strongest practical signal right now is still better tooling around how models call, sequence, and recover from actions.

items.title: OpenAI Operator upgraded to o3 reasoning model
items.summary: Operator web-browsing agent upgraded from GPT-4o to the o3 reasoning model for improved reliability and safety.
items.why: Reasoning models are being retrofitted into existing agent products, suggesting the next leap in agent reliability will come from thinking-before-acting architectures.

items.title: Claude for Finance — 10 financial agents for Cowork, Code, or headless API
items.summary: Anthropic released 10 financial agents deployable inside Claude Cowork, Claude Code, or as headless Managed Agents via API.
items.why: Vertical-specific agent packs are a preview of how most industries will first encounter agentic AI: pre-built, domain-tuned, and ready to wire into existing workflows.

items.title: Show HN: Statewright — visual state machines to make AI agents reliable
items.summary: Agents build state machines via MCP with visual editor, failure paths, retry loops, and approval gates.
items.why: The state-machine framing tackles one of the biggest agent pain points directly.

items.title: Show HN: Adam — an embeddable cross-platform AI agent library in C
items.summary: Described as 'the SQLite of agent frameworks': small, portable, tool calling, memory, voice, sessions, and self-evolving loops.
items.why: An embeddable C agent library signals demand for small, portable agent runtimes.

items.title: Show HN: Semble — code search for agents
items.summary: Code search for agents that uses an ingest layer optimized for agent navigation rather than simple grep.
items.why: Agent-native developer tools are emerging as a distinct category from traditional devtools.

items.title: Agentic sprawl is becoming a real organizational problem. What does responsible AI agent governance even look like?
items.summary: Discussion on governance frameworks as organizations deploy increasing numbers of autonomous agents.
items.why: Agent governance is becoming a first-class concern as enterprise adoption scales.

items.title: moonshotai/Kimi-K2.6 — native multimodal agentic model for long-horizon coding and swarm orchestration
items.summary: Open-source native multimodal agentic model for long-horizon coding, swarm-based task orchestration, and proactive autonomous execution.
items.why: Swarm-based task orchestration in an open model is a strong signal that multi-agent patterns are hardening in open-weight stacks.

items.title: rStar2-Agent-14B — agentic RL model for alignment, reasoning, and tool-use
items.summary: 14B model trained with agentic RL; generalizes to alignment, scientific reasoning, and tool-use.
items.why: Agentic RL at 14B parameters shows that reasoning and tool-use can be trained into compact models with high sample efficiency.

items.title: Open Agent Leaderboard — IBM Research + Hugging Face
items.meta: May 2026 · open framework for evaluating deployed agents
items.summary: New open framework for evaluating deployed agents beyond simple task scores.
items.why: Better agent evaluation is a bottleneck for the whole field; an open leaderboard from IBM and HF raises the bar.

items.title: AIDC-AI/Marco-DeepResearch-8B-GGUF
items.summary: gguf quantized deep-research agent information-seeking web-search verification react llama-cpp qwen3 text-generation en zh arxiv:2603.28376 base_model:AIDC-AI/Marco-DeepResearch-8B base_model:quantized:AIDC-AI/Marco-DeepResearch-8B license:apache-2.0 model-index endpoints_compatible region:us conversational
items.why: Compact “deep research” style models are getting easier to run and remix, which widens the range of people who can experiment with retrieval-heavy agent workflows.

items.title: yetone/native-feel-skill
items.summary: An Agent Skill for designing cross-platform desktop apps that feel native — distilled from Raycast's 2.0 deep-dive and reverse engineering of Raycast Beta.app. Eight architectural tenets, four-layer architecture, WebKit/WebView2 survival guide, 75-item ship audit.
items.why: Agent UX is moving beyond chat boxes — interface design is becoming part of the research frontier.

items.title: HermannBjorgvin/Clawdmeter
items.summary: ESP32 desk dashboard that shows Claude Code usage
items.why: This is playful but telling: once people start building physical dashboards for an agent, it usually means the tool has become part of their everyday workflow rather than a one-off demo.

items.title: DenisSergeevitch/agents-best-practices
items.summary: Provider-neutral Agent Skill for Codex, Claude Code, and agentic harness design.
items.why: The strongest practical signal right now is still better tooling around how models call, sequence, and recover from actions.

items.title: gi-dellav/zerostack
items.summary: Minimalistic coding agent written in Rust, optimized for memory footprint and performance
items.why: A lot of the quality gap in agents still comes down to state handling, durability, and clearer control flow.

items.title: nvidia/Hermes — self-improving agent framework for RTX and DGX Spark
items.meta: May 13, 2026 · local agent execution on NVIDIA hardware
items.summary: Self-improving AI agent framework designed to run locally on RTX PCs and DGX Spark.
items.why: When NVIDIA builds an agent framework for consumer GPUs, local agent execution is officially mainstream.

experimentQueue.title: Try a remote/async coding agent session
experimentQueue.why: Mistral's remote agents and Anthropic's dreaming both point to agents that work over long time horizons instead of synchronous chat.
experimentQueue.prompt: Set up a coding agent to run a multi-step task asynchronously, then inspect the trace after it completes. Note where it stalled, recovered, or needed human gate.

experimentQueue.title: Audit one packaged vertical agent workflow
experimentQueue.why: Claude for Finance and Alibaba's agentic shopping show agents being packaged for specific domains.
experimentQueue.prompt: Pick one vertical agent bundle, map its hidden assumptions, and note where trust, permissions, and failure recovery still feel weak.

experimentQueue.title: Prototype with a state-machine agent framework
experimentQueue.why: Statewright and similar tools are making agent workflows more legible and less fragile via explicit state machines.
experimentQueue.prompt: Build a small agent workflow using an explicit state machine (or visual editor if available) and compare reliability against an equivalent prompt-chain approach.

method: Targeted Hacker News searches for high-signal agentic AI posts from the last 7 days
Reddit weekly top-post scan in LocalLLaMA, ClaudeAI, and r/artificial filtered for practical agent-building signals
Hugging Face model search filtered toward fresh open-weight agent, deep-research, and Qwen-related releases
Major-lab RSS checks plus official-domain matches surfaced through direct news monitoring
GitHub repo and release scans for fresh agent tooling, dashboards, browsers, workspaces, and coding-agent updates
Manual curation of high-signal announcements from OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Mistral, Alibaba/Qwen, and NVIDIA
